prompt: Fetch the latest posts from the JSONPlaceholder API (GET https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts). Filter out any posts where the title contains the word 'qui'. Then post a summary message to a Slack channel called #api-digest that says how many posts remain and lists their titles. Configure all nodes as completely as possible and don't ask me for credentials, I'll set them up later.

scenarios.name: happy-path
scenarios.description: API returns posts, some contain 'qui' and get filtered, summary posted to Slack
scenarios.dataSetup: The HTTP Request node returns JSON objects with fields: id, userId, title, body. Some of them should have 'qui' in the title (e.g. 'qui est esse', 'nesciunt quid non qui'). Others should NOT contain 'qui'. The Slack node returns a success response.
scenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors. Posts containing 'qui' in the title are filtered out. The Slack message is posted to #api-digest and includes how many posts remain plus the titles of the remaining posts. None of the titles in the Slack message should contain the word 'qui'.

scenarios.name: empty-response
scenarios.description: The API returns an empty array
scenarios.dataSetup: The HTTP Request node returns an empty array []. The Slack node returns a success response.
scenarios.successCriteria: The workflow handles an empty API response without crashing. The Slack message is either posted with a 'no posts' message or the workflow completes gracefully without posting.

scenarios.name: all-filtered
scenarios.description: Every post contains 'qui' in the title and all are filtered out
scenarios.dataSetup: The HTTP Request node returns 3 posts, all with 'qui' in their titles. The Slack node returns a success response.
scenarios.successCriteria: The workflow handles the case where all items are filtered out. It should not crash or send an empty message to Slack.
